paragraphs.body: In the World, there are things we all want to achieve. Each road is a little bit harder for another, and each achievement plays a big part in that person’s life. How we get there matters, but why we get there is the background of it all. We do things for certain reasons, whether it’s for family or to prove to ourselves that we can do so. For me, that’s getting a diploma from The University of Pittsburgh. This accomplishment is way more than just an achievement it’s a blueprint for those in my family behind me, it’s to prove to them that things can be done when you work hard for it.

annotations.comment: Is there a better introduction form that you might use to more immediately engage your reader? Narrative perhaps?
annotations.context_left: another, and each achievement plays a big part in that person’s life. How we get there matters,

paragraphs.body: To most, it’s just paper, but to me, it’s a symbol of doing what many around me have not—a moment of triumph as I walk across a stage in front of nearly 2,000 people. Among them are my family and friends, their voices rising above the crowd, calling my name, cheering me on as I take hold of a piece of paper that bears my name. But it’s not just any paper; it’s a representation of years of late nights, early mornings, sacrifices, and relentless determination. This single sheet captures the essence of my accomplishment in earning a college degree. The emotions it stirs are overwhelming pride, joy, and even tears as I reflect on what it’ll take to get there. How can one simple piece of paper hold such profound meaning? It doesn’t just signify my academic success; it embodies the long hours of effort, the resilience to overcome challenges, and the unwavering belief in myself. When I graduated high school, a similar paper brought me joy, but this one will bring me even more. This one is different. This one feels almost sacred, like a blessing sent from above, a reward for persistence and faith. It’s worth more to me than any dollar bill, not just because of its value but because of what it represents: my name, my journey, and my honor as a graduate of the University of Pittsburgh. Unlike a dollar bill, which carries a president’s name, this paper carries mine as a testament to my achievement and my legacy. This paper isn’t just the finish line of a four-year journey toward a bachelor’s degree; it’s a milestone that will never lose its value. Like currency, it holds endless worth, but its significance is priceless. It is not merely paper; it’s a reflection of who I am and who I’ve become.

annotations.comment: Is this your writing? It doesn't sound like your voice or syntax. Let's talk.
annotations.context_left: have not—a moment of triumph as I walk across a stage in front of nearly 2,000 people.

paragraphs.body: I will be the conqueror of these challenges, What I’ve learned is that the word conquers, depending on the person, could be positive or negative. Sir Edmund’s quote, “It is not the mountain we conquer but ourselves,” is something that hit me because it wasn’t the idea of Mount Et he was worried it was his state of mind and being able to push himself to be the first person to reach the top. That’s what I believe my life to be it’s not about where I'm going but whether I am willing to get there without giving up, no matter the obstacles, and no matter the struggle, am I able to reach Mount Everest. Right now, I am getting through the obstacles, but I know there are days ahead when I want to give us. I want to be able to conquer everything that stands in my way. To conquer doesn’t always have to mean violence. Conquering can be shown in another way other than force and oppression. To conquer can be demonstrated by overcoming obstacles that gave you trouble, to conquer can mean to complete a task you gave you all too. Conquering gives much more than just the feelings of fear and of a controller. But it also implies triumph, growth, and perseverance. In which you show such a type of resilience and determination to conquer. Conquerors overcame things through force, but when you cook yourself some breakfast, you become a conqueror as well, you conquer your fear of starving, you conquer the task of cooking a meal, and you conquer the idea of being a chef lol. Either way, being a conqueror is so much more than just force it is your will to get something done, anyone can be a conqueror it doesn’t take an army or a crown it just takes a challenge or goal and your will to complete such a thing.

annotations.comment: This feels like the beginning of a definition paragraph, but leverage this paragraph by defining your term with concrete and specific details from your own life, ideas, and experience. Define it in a way no one else in the world can.  How are you going to "conquer" the world, specifically?

paragraphs.body: It was an early, early morning, one of the days when it was still dark outside but says 6:00 am, I woke up with a crust in my eye, not knowing why I was up so early, with the wind blowing in my room and the silence was heighten as I'm having one of the m morning daydreams of the ceiling while lying in my bed. This was one of those mornings where your thoughts were loud, and all you could do was listen what my thoughts were saying was that I needed to do my best to become the best person I could be. I thought deeply about this thought. It is bigger than us when it comes to conquering and overcoming odds because you have no idea who is watching, and having an impact on someone is a dream that everyone should have because we want to be remembered, ideally for inspiring and being someone we can look up to. Some people must overcome to get where they want to go; they are not motivated to keep going once they hit a bump, especially when they have no one to turn to in their journey. It was like that for me most of my life until I figured out what I wanted, and it took multiple influences on me to understand the thought process I must have to get where I want to go. Multiple mentors, uncles, brothers, friends, and programs help me build the mindset of becoming a conqueror so that I can make the same impact on the younger generation.

paragraphs.body: We can all achieve big things in this world, even if we think these things are out of reach, some people have done it before us, so we look at those who took the same path so that we have the inspiration we need to know that we can conquer the same things. Even if the paths are different, we know that things can be done, and how I plan on getting my diploma is bigger than me, and it’s not just a paper but a blueprint.

annotations.text: bigger than me, and it’s not just a paper but a blueprint.
annotations.comment: a blueprint of what, exactly? What is your life's plan? Do you think the answers to these questions could become a part of your definition paragraph?

So many fascinating insights and repetitions in this essay! I'm drawn to so many places of heat and energy, and I'm particularly taken with the confidence of your voice and tone when you talk about the word "conquer." That said, there is one major section of this essay that I believe you didn't write on your own. It's a metaphor but not the kind that we're developing, and the language/syntax of the paragraph is vague and general in a way that doesn't feel particular to your voice, ideas, and experience. We'll need to discuss that. I also don't see a descriptive paragraph, yet, which is a required paragraph type for the project. Your definition and narrative paragraphs are also not yet fully formed.
